classifications.phrase: increase the cash rate target by 25 basis points
inflation has fallen substantially since its peak in 2022
it picked up materially in the second half of 2025
some of the increase in inflation reflects greater capacity pressures
sharply higher fuel prices, which, if sustained, will add to inflation
material risk that inflation will remain above target for longer
Growth in private demand strengthened substantially more than was expected
unemployment rate has been a little lower than expected
measures of labour underutilisation remain at low rates
inflationary pressures picked up materially in the second half of 2025
labour market has tightened a little recently
capacity pressures are slightly greater than previously assessed
could add to global and domestic inflation
inflation is likely to remain above target for some time
risks have tilted further to the upside, including to inflation expectations
It was therefore appropriate to increase the cash rate target

payload.rationale: The RBA signals a hawkish stance through rate increases driven by material inflation risks, capacity pressures, tight labour market conditions, and upside risks to inflation expectations.
